attributes.description: Physical location of the meeting (address, room name, venue).
attributes.name: location
attributes.usage: Drives logistics coordination, calendar invitations, and travel-time estimation. Distinct from videoMeetingUrl on the video-meeting subclass.

attributes.name: attendees
attributes.usage: Multi-attendee list. Drives meeting-attendance reporting and contact-engagement-frequency calculations. Salesforce uses EventRelation junction; EspoCRM uses link-multiple.

attributes.name: agenda
attributes.usage: Drives meeting-prep workflow. Often populated from a meeting-template before the meeting; reviewed and updated as outcome notes after.

attributes.description: Post-meeting summary of what was discussed and what's next.
attributes.name: outcome
attributes.usage: Captured after the meeting. Drives next-action assignment, follow-up tasks, and CRM activity-history continuity. The most-read field on completed meeting records.

business_context: Meeting records capture scheduled in-person interactions: client meetings, lunch meetings, conference attendance, on-site visits, board meetings. They differ from generic Activity records in carrying location data (where the meeting happens) and a multi-attendee list (who's there). Modern CRMs increasingly distinguish in-person Meetings from Video Meetings (separate subclass) because the workflows differ: in-person involves physical-location coordination, travel time, and often expense tracking; video involves URL provisioning, platform selection, and recording management. Most surveyed systems don't natively split these — they have a single Meeting / Event entity that serves both purposes with optional location or URL fields. The catalog documents both as separate subclasses to make the distinction explicit and capture the cross-system mapping at deploy time. When interviewing clients, surface "Meeting" by asking whether they need to track in-person events distinct from video calls (often relevant for field-sales orgs, board management, donor-cultivation events) or whether one combined Meeting concept suffices.
name: Meeting (Activity subclass)
purpose: An in-person meeting or event scheduled with one or more contacts. A subclass of Activity that adds meeting-specific delta attributes: physical location, attendee list, agenda, outcome notes. Distinct from Video Meeting (which has a meeting URL and platform metadata).

systems.name: Meetings
systems.notes: Attio Meetings auto-created from calendar sync (Google / Outlook). Surfaces on Person and Company timelines. Recognizes virtual-meeting URLs but doesn't natively split in-person from virtual.

systems.name: Interaction (subtype=In-Person)
systems.notes: Bloomerang Interaction with In-Person type. Captures Date, Description, Constituent.

systems.name: Activity (activity_type=Meeting)
systems.notes: CiviCRM Activity with activity_type=Meeting. Doesn't natively distinguish in-person from virtual.

systems.name: Meeting
systems.notes: First-class Meeting entity with name, status (Planned / Held / Not Held), date_start, date_end, parent (polymorphic), users (link-multiple for attendees), description.

systems.name: Meeting (Engagement subtype)
systems.notes: HubSpot Meeting engagement covers both in-person and video. Includes meeting_outcome, location, internal_notes, body. Video meetings carry meeting URL via integrations.

systems.name: Event
systems.notes: Salesforce Event object covers all meeting types (in-person and virtual). Includes Location field; no first-class virtual-meeting URL field but commonly added as custom.

systems.name: Event
systems.notes: Same as Salesforce. Donor cultivation meetings, board meetings, and donor events all use the Event object.
